title: Executive Interview with Chris Morrison, VP Product Marketing at Agile Analog - Semiwiki
gist: Agile Analog has joined GlobalPlatform's newly launched Pavona initiative — a community-governed, open-source secure-silicon distribution backed by Meta, Qualcomm, Analog Devices and zeroRISC — contributing configurable analog anti-tamper IP as the physical-security layer wrapping Pavona's digital roots of trust and post-quantum crypto stack.

insights.claim: GlobalPlatform has launched Pavona, a community-governed open-source silicon distribution that provides chip designers with certification-ready secure silicon IP components and reference designs for embedded and high-performance systems.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: A new standards-body-backed open IP distribution reshapes the secure-silicon supply chain; watch which IP vendors get designed into its curated library.
insights.quote: GlobalPlatform recently launched Pavona, a community-governed open-source silicon distribution, to provide chip designers with certification-ready secure silicon IP components and reference designs

insights.claim: Pavona targets production-quality, certification-ready IP aligned with FIPS 140-3 and Common Criteria requirements, offering a composable framework for building secure-by-default silicon.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Certification-readiness against FIPS 140-3 and Common Criteria lowers the compliance barrier for adopters, a key adoption driver for security-sensitive markets.
insights.quote: aligned with FIPS 140-3 and Common Criteria certification requirements
insights.locator: This open-source silicon distribution aims to deliver

insights.claim: Pavona differentiates from prior open-source silicon efforts by moving beyond single-use monolithic core designs to a modular platform with a composition engine and curated IP library that scales across datacenter servers, AI accelerators and IoT devices.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: A composable, multi-market model (vs. single cores) signals broader addressable use across AI and datacenter silicon — larger TAM than earlier open-core projects.
insights.quote: Pavona introduces a modular distribution model with a composition engine and curated IP library.
insights.locator: Previous open source initiatives have focused primarily on individual cores

insights.claim: Pavona's founding members include Meta, Qualcomm, Analog Devices, zeroRISC and Agile Analog, spanning IP providers, semiconductor companies, software suppliers, standards bodies and end-product stakeholders.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Backing from hyperscaler Meta and Qualcomm plus zeroRISC (OpenTitan commercialization) lends the effort real commercial weight; track whether members ship Pavona-based parts.
insights.quote: Agile Analog is delighted to join major semiconductor players, including Meta, Qualcomm, Analog Devices and zeroRISC, as one of the founding members
insights.locator: Hosted by GlobalPlatform, Pavona is supported by a Governing Board

insights.claim: Pavona is governed by a Governing Board of funding members while an independent Technical Steering Committee holds sole responsibility for the technical roadmap and oversight.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Separation of funding from technical control mirrors mature open-source governance, reducing single-vendor capture risk and improving adopter trust.
insights.quote: an independent Technical Steering Committee holds sole responsibility for the project's technical roadmap and oversight

insights.claim: Pavona launches with two silicon-proven, taped-out reference designs — a standalone chip root of trust and an integrated root of trust for chiplet architectures — fabricated at TSMC 3nm.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Taped-out silicon at TSMC 3nm (not just specs/simulation) plus a chiplet root of trust signals readiness for advanced-node and chiplet-based designs — a credibility differentiator.
insights.quote: Pavona launches with two successfully taped-out reference designs: a standalone chip root of trust and an integrated root of trust for chiplet architectures fabricated at TSMC 3nm.
insights.locator: Pavona launches with two successfully taped-out reference designs

insights.claim: Pavona offers the first openly available silicon distribution featuring a production-grade post-quantum cryptography stack for embedded systems, using PQC algorithms including ML-KEM and ML-DSA.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: A production-grade PQC (ML-KEM/ML-DSA) open stack positions Pavona early on the quantum-threat migration curve, a growing procurement requirement for defense and datacenter buyers.
insights.quote: Pavona also offers the first openly available silicon distribution featuring a production-grade post-quantum cryptography (PQC) stack for embedded systems
insights.locator: Pavona also offers the first openly available silicon distribution

insights.claim: Agile Analog positions configurable analog IP as the critical bridge between the digital security domain and the physical world, providing the physical security layer that protects Pavona's digital security stack.
insights.speaker: Chris Morrison
insights.so_what: Framing analog as the mandatory physical-security wrapper around digital roots of trust is the core investment thesis for Agile Analog's relevance in secure silicon.
insights.quote: Analog IP is the critical bridge between the digital security domain and the physical world.
insights.locator: Why did Agile Analog want to be involved?

insights.claim: Agile Analog argues digital security is only as good as the hardware it sits on, so its agileSecure anti-tamper IP protects deployments from supply, clock, thermal and electromagnetic attacks.
insights.speaker: Chris Morrison
insights.so_what: Positioning anti-tamper as protecting against side-channel/physical attack vectors expands the security spend beyond crypto cores into analog IP — Agile Analog's monetization angle.
insights.quote: digital security is only as good as the hardware it sits on
insights.locator: How does this initiative fit with Agile Analog's portfolio

insights.claim: Agile Analog highlights its process-portable analog capabilities, including tamper prevention and tamper detection IP, as its contribution to an open ecosystem with high security rigor.
insights.speaker: Chris Morrison
insights.so_what: Process-portability lets the same analog IP move across foundries/nodes, a scalability and margin advantage for a customizable-IP licensing model.
insights.quote: We are excited to bring our process-portable analog capabilities into an open ecosystem with this level of security rigor.

insights.claim: agileSecure anti-tamper security IP has become a key part of Agile Analog's product portfolio, and the company plans to keep building out its roadmap with additional protection.
insights.speaker: Chris Morrison
insights.so_what: Anti-tamper is a strategic growth pillar, not a side product, for Agile Analog — expect further product releases in physical hardware security.
insights.quote: Our agileSecure anti-tamper security IP has become a key part of our product portfolio
insights.locator: How does this initiative fit with Agile Analog's portfolio
